question: Which component is located directly above the modem in the enclosure?

text_chunks: Install vents on left and right side of enclosure. Place aluminum plate on bench. Attach Netgear switch using zip ties in top right of plate. The CAT5 ports will point to the left.

Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure. Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench. Apply a zip tie and label with 'PV IN' around the PV+/- pigtail wires. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.

TURN OFF ALL BREAKERS. 26. Complete continuity check procedure DC004. Attach modem to mezzanine plate using QTY two (2) 10-32 socket head cap screws. Attach modem to 4-40 hex standoffs using QTY four 4-40 socket head cap screws. Install CAT5 cables between the modem, web relay, and the Netgear switch. Attach power cable to modem

Split PoE Injector wire and connect to web relay Relay 1. The C terminal goes to the distribution block. The NO terminal goes to the PoE injector. Complete the power wiring for the modem cable. The white , red, and green wires go to the positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

text_chunks: Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block). Complete power wiring for PoE Injector . Leave extra slack in the PoE injector positive wire as it will be split for the web relay later.

Split PoE Injector wire and connect to web relay Relay 1. The C terminal goes to the distribution block. The NO terminal goes to the PoE injector. Complete the power wiring for the modem cable. The white , red, and green wires go to the positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure. Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench. Apply a zip tie and label with 'PV IN' around the PV+/- pigtail wires. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.

text_chunks: TM001(02) Flashing Light Video Monitor Assembly Instructions Materials Required Procedure Kitting Instructions FLVM Assembly Instructions 2-4-2026.docx Materials Required 1. 18 AWG 90C wire (red and black) 2. Zip ties 3. Label maker 4. WAGO 221-415 5-position lever nuts 5. WAGO 221-612 2-position lever nuts (10 AWG) 6. Wire cutter/snips 7. 3/32' hex drive bit 8. 1/8' hex drive bit 9. 5/32 hex drive bit 10. Precision flat head screwdriver Small and large Phillips screwdriver Procedure 1. Drill holes in enclosure for antenna, 2x vents, and 1' NPT conduit fitting. Install the antenna base into the top enclosure hole. Be sure to lubricate the o-ring before tightening.

Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure. Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench. Apply a zip tie and label with 'PV IN' around the PV+/- pigtail wires. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.

TURN OFF ALL BREAKERS. 26. Complete continuity check procedure DC004. Attach modem to mezzanine plate using QTY two (2) 10-32 socket head cap screws. Attach modem to 4-40 hex standoffs using QTY four 4-40 socket head cap screws. Install CAT5 cables between the modem, web relay, and the Netgear switch. Attach power cable to modem
Install vents on left and right side of enclosure. Place aluminum plate on bench. Attach Netgear switch using zip ties in top right of plate. The CAT5 ports will point to the left.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

Complete wiring for solar controller LOAD output . This includes the LOAD +, LOAD -, LOAD +_SW, and J+ wiring from the schematic. Use a DMM to check the polarity of the two barrel jack connectors. The outside of the barrel jack is negative. Mark the positive wire with a piece of red tape.

Complete power wiring for the solar controller BATT input. This includes the BATT+, BATT-, and BATT +_SW wiring from the wiring diagram. 15. Complete power wiring for solar controller PV input . This includes the PV +, PV -, and PV+_SW wiring from the wiring diagram. Ensure that you use 10AWG (PN 221-612) WAGO connectors on the PV pigtails. The pigtails should be at least 12' long.

A list of devices will be displayed (you may be asked to turn on Bluetooth). Select the device based on the serial number that was noted during production. If this is not available, the serial number and factory pin can be found on top of the solar controller. Pair with the device when prompted by using the factory pin.

question: How many 5-position WAGO connectors are installed in each DIN rail carrier?
ground_truth: The top DIN rail carrier has two 5-position WAGO connectors, and the bottom carrier has one.
predicted_answer: Two (2) 5-position WAGOs in one carrier, one (1) 5-position WAGO in the second carrier.
raw_answer:
Two (2) 5-position WAGOs in one carrier, one (1) 5-position WAGO in the second carrier.

Sources: [altumint_tm001_02_flashing_light_video_monitor_assembly_instructions_p05]

text_chunks: Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

Attach DIN rail to middle right of plate using QTY four (4) 10-32 hex button head screws. Attach circuit breakers to DIN rail. From left to right, the order is 6A -> 13A -> 2A Mark the circuit breakers , from left to right, as PV --> BATT --> LOAD using a permanent marker.

NOTES: 1. USE 18AWG 90°C WIRE UNLESS OTHERWISE SPECIFIED. Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure. Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench. Apply a zip tie and label with 'PV IN' around the PV+/- pigtail wires. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.

The white , red, and green wires go to the positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.

Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block). Attach DIN rail to middle right of plate using QTY four (4) 10-32 hex button head screws. Attach circuit breakers to DIN rail. From left to right, the order is 6A -> 13A -> 2A Mark the circuit breakers , from left to right, as PV --> BATT --> LOAD using a permanent marker.

Split PoE Injector wire and connect to web relay Relay 1. The C terminal goes to the distribution block. The NO terminal goes to the PoE injector. Complete the power wiring for the modem cable. The white , red, and green wires go to the positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.

Disconnect camera from CAT5 cable. Coil CAT5 camera cable into enclosure and secure with zip tie. Secure battery strap to aluminum plate. Return camera to its cardboard box. Remove batteries from electronics enclosure.

Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block). Complete power wiring for PoE Injector . Leave extra slack in the PoE injector positive wire as it will be split for the web relay later.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

FLVM Configuration Instructions 2-5-2026.docx Materials Required Laptop or PC with Google Chrome Browser CAT5 cable QTY 4 - Tycon TSBAT12-10L-WT Lithium Battery Android or iPhone cell phone Procedure Connect batteries to the battery spade connectors Turn BATT breaker to on (up) position Turn LOAD breaker to on (up) position Camera Remove camera from box and connect it to the CAT5 cable that is coming out of the POE injector.

TM001(02) Flashing Light Video Monitor Assembly Instructions Materials Required Procedure Kitting Instructions FLVM Assembly Instructions 2-4-2026.docx Materials Required 1. 18 AWG 90C wire (red and black) 2. Zip ties 3. Label maker 4. WAGO 221-415 5-position lever nuts 5. WAGO 221-612 2-position lever nuts (10 AWG) 6. Wire cutter/snips 7. 3/32' hex drive bit 8. 1/8' hex drive bit 9. 5/32 hex drive bit 10. Precision flat head screwdriver Small and large Phillips screwdriver Procedure 1. Drill holes in enclosure for antenna, 2x vents, and 1' NPT conduit fitting. Install the antenna base into the top enclosure hole. Be sure to lubricate the o-ring before tightening.

TM002(04) FLVM Configuration Instructions Purpose Materials Required Procedure Camera Web Relay Modem Solar Controller Testing Finishing Up Purpose The instructions contain info for configuring the web relay, modem, camera, and solar controller as part of the Flashing Light Video Monitor. FLVM Configuration Instructions 2-5-2026.docx Materials Required Laptop or PC with Google Chrome Browser CAT5 cable QTY 4 - Tycon TSBAT12-10L-WT Lithium Battery Android or iPhone cell phone Procedure Connect batteries to the battery spade connectors Turn BATT breaker to on (up) position Turn LOAD breaker to on (up) position Camera Remove camera from box and connect it to the CAT5 cable that is coming out of the POE injector.

Before placing the solar charge controller on the plate, make a note of the factory pin and serial number printed on the label on the top of the device. This will be used later to configure settings with the VictronConnect app. Attach solar charge controller to top left of plate using QTY four (4) 6-32 socket head cap screws. Attach PoE injector to middle left of plate using QTY four (4) 4-40 phillips truss head screws.

If you are unsure how to complete this step, please contact your supervisor or the IT department. Connect to web relay at http://192.168.1.100/ User: admin Pass: admin Navigate to the Access Settings page Change the Password and Confirm Password to 0pTo4600 Change Web Server Port to 5000 Click Save.

Change your computerʼs IP address to 192.168.0.150 Confirm you can connect and login to the web relay at You will see the login screen below: http://192.168.0.200:5000/ Modem Connect to the modem via the LAN at https://192.168.0.1:9443/ Login with the following: User: user Pass: #0pTo4600# Navigate to Security > Port Forwarding Add the following entries to the table: Click the Apply button. Click OK. Click the Reboot button. Solar Controller On your cell phone, go to the app store and download the VictronConnect app.

Navigate to System > Users . Click the 'Add Userʼ button and create an account with the following credentials: User: admin Pass: 0p T o4600 Navigate to System > Network. Turn off the slides for Bonj our and UPnP .

Click OK on any menu that pops up. Close the app to disconnect from the solar controller. Testing Connect to the web relay over the internet at http://<modem_IP_address>:4000 and login using: User: admin Pass: 0pTo4600 Click the IO Ports Control menu . Turn on the camera by click the checkbox: Connect to the camera over the internet at https://<modem_IP_address>:1200 . Confirm that you can view the cameraʼs web interface. Testing is completed. Finishing Up Turn LOAD circuit breaker to off (down) position. Turn BATT circuit breaker to off (down) position. Disconnect laptop CAT5 cable from Netgear switch.

Connect laptop with a CAT5 cable to a spare port on the Netgear switch. Change the laptopʼs ethernet IP address to 192 . 168 . 0. 150 Run AXIS IP utility. The cameraʼs IP address will be shown. Click the IP address to open the web interface. On the next page, set the password as 0pTo4600, accept the license agreement, and click ' Add Userʼ.

question: How many WAGO distribution blocks are shown in the diagram?
ground_truth: Three WAGO distribution blocks are shown.
